name: Ramesh Gowda
history_summary: Admitted for uncontrolled blood sugar. BP runs high at baseline. Two prior admissions this year for hyperglycemia. Stable on current medication for 4 days.

name: Lakshmi Devi
history_summary: COPD exacerbation last month, on maintenance inhalers. Baseline SpO2 is LOW-NORMAL for her (90-92%); do not panic at 91%, but a drop below 88% is significant deterioration.

name: Abdul Karim
history_summary: Heart attack in 2024 with stent placement. On beta-blocker so baseline HR is low (60-68). Chest pain, HR above 110, or new irregular rhythm is a red flag for this patient.

name: Sunita Sharma
history_summary: Day 2 after appendix surgery, recovering well. Mild fever up to 37.5 is expected post-op; above 38.5 suggests infection. Watch for rising HR with falling BP (internal bleeding).

name: Venkatesh Rao
history_summary: CKD stage 3 with anemia; tires easily, baseline HR slightly high. On diuretics — dehydration risk. Confusion or HR above 120 needs urgent review given his age.

name: Meena Kumari
history_summary: 32 weeks pregnant with gestational hypertension. BP above 150/100, severe headache, or visual changes = pre-eclampsia risk, treat as emergency. Baseline HR elevated as expected in pregnancy.

name: Krishnan Nair
history_summary: Community-acquired pneumonia, day 4 of antibiotics, improving. SpO2 climbing back from 92 to 94. A new fever spike or SpO2 drop below 92 suggests treatment failure.

name: Fatima Begum
history_summary: Moderate persistent asthma, admitted after a flare triggered by a chest infection. Rising respiratory rate is her earliest deterioration sign, before SpO2 falls. NEVER give aspirin.

name: Basavaraj Patil
history_summary: Liver disease with fluid in the abdomen; baseline BP is LOW (105-112 systolic) — normal for him. But a further drop below 95 systolic, fever (infection of the fluid), or confusion is an emergency.

name: Anjali Joshi
history_summary: Dengue day 5 — the critical plasma-leak window. Fever settling can be deceptive: rising HR with narrowing pulse pressure (bp_sys minus bp_dia under 25) signals dengue shock. Platelets 48k this morning. No NSAIDs.
